meta.title: CrewAI Explained
meta.subtitle: The open-source framework for orchestrating multi-agent AI teams
meta.author: Imbila.AI

summary: CrewAI is an open-source Python framework for orchestrating teams of AI agents, each with a specific role, goal, and toolset. Built from scratch (independent of LangChain), it provides six core abstractions — Agents, Tasks, Crews, Tools, Flows, and Memory — to choreograph multi-agent collaboration. The commercial Agent Management Platform (AMP) adds visual studio, tracing, and enterprise deployment.

products.name: Agents
products.description: Each agent has a role, goal, and backstory that shape its behaviour. Optionally assign an LLM, enable reasoning mode, grant delegation rights, and toggle memory. LLM-agnostic — works with OpenAI, Anthropic, Gemini, Ollama, or any provider.

products.name: Tasks
products.description: A Task defines a unit of work with a clear description, expected output format, and the agent assigned to it. Tasks can depend on other tasks for automatic context passing. Supports structured outputs via Pydantic models.

products.name: Crews
products.description: A Crew brings agents and tasks together under a process model. Sequential process runs tasks in order. Hierarchical process assigns a manager agent that delegates dynamically. Supports planning mode, shared memory, and a manager LLM.

products.name: Tools
products.description: Agents use tools to interact with the outside world — web search, file operations, API calls, database queries, code execution. Ships with 100+ built-in tools and supports custom tools via a simple Python decorator pattern.

products.name: Flows
products.description: Event-driven, deterministic orchestration above Crews — conditional branching, state management, parallel execution, and error handling. A Flow is the project plan; Crews are the teams executing it. Uses @start() and @listen() decorators.

products.name: Memory
products.description: Four memory types: short-term (within a run), long-term (across runs), entity memory (about specific subjects), and contextual memory (shared between agents). Lets agents recall past decisions and build on prior work.

open_source.description: The core Python framework — MIT-licensed, standalone, and free to self-host. Includes agents, tasks, crews, flows, tools, and memory.

platform.description: The commercial Agent Management Platform. Adds CrewAI Studio (visual no-code editor), real-time tracing, agent training, RBAC, serverless deployment, and enterprise integrations. Cloud or on-prem.

education.description: Free courses at learn.crewai.com, including a collaboration with Andrew Ng on multi-agent systems. Over 100,000 developers have completed certification.

use_when: Your workflow has natural role separation — research, analysis, writing, QA — and would benefit from specialised agents handling each phase.
You need multi-step pipelines where output from one agent feeds into the next, with dependencies and handoffs between stages.
You want LLM-agnostic orchestration — assigning different models to different agents based on cost, latency, or capability requirements.
Your team works in Python and wants opinionated abstractions over building orchestration from scratch with raw API calls.
skip_when: A single agent with good tools can handle your entire task. Adding multi-agent overhead to a simple Q&A bot or document summariser will cost more and deliver no quality improvement.
You need maximum control over state transitions and execution paths — LangGraph's explicit graph model gives you more precision at the cost of more boilerplate.
Budget is tight and every LLM call counts. Multi-agent systems multiply token usage. A four-agent crew might make 8-12x the API calls of a single agent.
You need real-time latency under 2 seconds. Agent delegation, tool use, and inter-agent communication add latency that makes sub-second responses impractical.

use_cases.title: Lead Enrichment & Outreach
use_cases.description: DocuSign uses CrewAI agents to extract, consolidate, and evaluate lead data from multiple internal systems — cutting research time from hours to minutes and improving email conversion rates.

use_cases.title: Code Generation & Audit
use_cases.description: PwC deployed CrewAI agentic workflows and improved code-generation accuracy from 10% to 70%, significantly reducing turnaround time on software deliverables.

use_cases.title: Curriculum Design
use_cases.description: General Assembly automated lesson content and instructor guide generation with agent crews, streamlining and scaling their curriculum design process.

use_cases.title: Lead Scoring & Prioritisation
use_cases.description: Gelato uses agents to enrich leads with data on company size, printer infrastructure, and revenue — improving quality and prioritisation in their sales pipeline.

use_cases.title: Legacy System Integration
use_cases.description: IBM combined CrewAI with WatsonX.AI to coordinate legacy and modern systems, using the agentic framework to bridge between IBM's foundation models and existing infrastructure.

use_cases.title: Ticket Resolution
use_cases.description: Piracanjuba replaced legacy RPA tooling with CrewAI agent crews, improving both response time and accuracy for customer support ticket resolution.

timeline.title: Open-Source Launch
timeline.detail: Joao Moura, then Director of AI Engineering at Clearbit (acquired by HubSpot), releases CrewAI on GitHub. Trends to #1 on GitHub trending, hitting 4,000+ stars rapidly.

timeline.title: Company Founded
timeline.detail: CrewAI officially launches as a company with Moura as CEO and Rob Bailey as COO. Enterprise customers including Oracle start calling.

timeline.title: 150 Enterprise Customers
timeline.detail: Within six months of incorporating, CrewAI attracts 150 enterprise customers. Andrew Ng collaborates on a course about multi-agent systems.

timeline.title: $18M Funding & Enterprise Launch
timeline.detail: Series A led by Insight Partners. Angel investors include Andrew Ng and Dharmesh Shah (HubSpot CTO). CrewAI Enterprise launches with studio, tracing, and managed deployment. Framework surpasses 10M+ agents/month.

timeline.title: Flows & AMP Platform
timeline.detail: CrewAI introduces Flows — event-driven state machines that complement Crews. Agent Management Platform launches with visual builder, on-prem deployment, and swarm handoff patterns. Stars pass 40k.

timeline.title: 1.4B+ Automations
timeline.detail: Over 1.4 billion agentic automations processed. Adoption reaches approximately 60% of Fortune 500 companies. Framework stabilises around Crews + Flows dual architecture.

pricing.paid: Commercial licensing — includes Studio, tracing, RBAC, serverless deployment

code_examples.title: Define an Agent
code_examples.code:
Agent(
  role="Market Researcher",
  goal="Find competitor pricing data",
  backstory="Senior analyst at a strategy consultancy",
  tools=[search_tool, scrape_tool],
  reasoning=True
)

code_examples.title: Define a Task
code_examples.code:
Task(
  description="Analyse top 5 competitors' pricing...",
  expected_output="Comparison table as JSON",
  agent=researcher_agent
)
